data.title: Kalshi and Polymarket to offer perpetual futures; Trump extends ceasefire
data.description:
Headline

▌Kalshi and Polymarket to Offer Perpetual Futures Trading

On April 22, following competitor Kalshi's plan to offer cryptocurrency perpetual futures trading, Polymarket also began expanding its perpetual futures trading business.

▌Trump Announces Extension of Ceasefire Against Iran

On April 22, US President Trump posted on "Real Social Media" on the afternoon of April 21 local time, stating that at the request of the Pakistani Army Chief of Staff and Prime Minister, the US would suspend military strikes against Iran, extend the ceasefire period, and require Iran to first submit a unified negotiation proposal. During this period, the US military will continue to maintain a naval blockade against Iran while maintaining a state of military readiness. He stated that the ceasefire will continue until Iran submits a proposal and completes negotiations, "regardless of the outcome."

Policy

▌Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh supports crypto inclusion in the financial system; lawmakers raise regulatory questions

On April 22, Kevin Warsh stated at a Senate hearing that digital assets "have become part of the U.S. financial system" and support their inclusion to provide investors with more opportunities and protection. This statement is seen as a policy signal of a generally open approach to the crypto industry should he become the Federal Reserve Chair. Warsh has previously stated that Bitcoin is "an important asset that helps policymakers." However, Elizabeth Warren expressed concerns during the hearing, mentioning the potential risks of "sock puppets" in the crypto space and emphasizing the need for stronger regulation and prevention of abuse.

▌SEC Chairman: Moving Forward to Establish a Regulatory Framework for Digital Assets and Proposing the "A-C-T" Strategy

In his keynote speech at the Economic Club of Washington, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins pointed out that in his first year in office, he has pushed the SEC back to its core mission of "protecting investors, maintaining market order, and promoting capital formation," and criticized past regulations for being overly expansionary, increasing market friction and uncertainty. Paul Atkins added that the SEC is accelerating the establishment of a clear regulatory foundation for digital assets, collaborating with other regulatory agencies and Congress, and has proposed the "A-C-T" strategy, which includes advancing regulatory modernization, clarifying regulatory boundaries, and transforming the rule system based on fundamental principles, to enhance the competitiveness of the U.S. market and consolidate its position as a global investment and business environment.

...

▌New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase and Gemini, Alleging Prediction Markets Constitute "Illegal Gambling"

On April 21, Letitia James filed a lawsuit against Coinbase and Gemini, accusing them of providing "disguised gambling" services through their prediction market platforms, violating New York state law. Regulators argue that such transactions based on event outcomes (such as sports or elections) are essentially gambling activities, particularly questioning the allowance for users aged 18 to 21, while New York law stipulates a minimum age of 21 for sports betting. Prosecutors are seeking substantial fines and forfeiture of proceeds, including at least $2.2 billion from Coinbase and at least $1.2 billion from Gemini. Neither company has officially responded, but Coinbase stated that prediction markets are regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, implying that their legality falls under federal jurisdiction.

▌Philippine Securities and Exchange Commission Issues Investment Warning for 7 Crypto Trading Platforms

On April 21, the Philippine Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) issued an investor warning, advising the public against investing in seven cryptocurrency trading platforms: dYdX, Aevo, gTrade, Pacifica, Orderly, Deriv, and Ostium. The SEC stated that these platforms are not registered with the Commission and have not obtained the necessary authorizations required by the Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) framework. The SEC also warned that individuals promoting these platforms in the Philippines could face criminal charges, including a fine of up to 5,000,000 Philippine pesos (approximately US$89,000) or imprisonment for up to 21 years.

...

▌Iran Agrees to Suspension of Military Fighting, But the War Is Not Over

On April 22, local time, in response to Trump's statement regarding extending the ceasefire, Iranian state television stated that Iran has become the victor on the battlefield. Control of the Strait of Hormuz is an extremely valuable bargaining chip that Iran has gained in this war. Iran has agreed to a suspension of military fighting, but the war is not over. At the same time, Iranian state television also pointed out that Iran must be wary of any statements suggesting that it must participate in negotiations or its enemies will attack it. Even if the naval blockade is lifted, Iran's participation in negotiations must be conditional on not raising any issues that infringe upon Iran's independence and dignity, with the primary focus being Iran's defense and missile capabilities, as well as its nuclear capabilities and technology.

... (CCTV) On April 22, the Iranian negotiating team announced to the US through Pakistani intermediaries that, for various reasons, they would not travel to Islamabad, Pakistan on the 22nd and currently have no possibility of participating in the negotiations. Sources indicated that after Pakistan intervened to mediate and requested a ceasefire from the US, Iran accepted the ceasefire agreement and agreed to conduct subsequent negotiations to end the war based on a ten-point framework it proposed, which had already been accepted by the US. Pakistan also explicitly stated that the US accepted this framework. However, in the following days, the US began to renege on its promises. Furthermore, in the first round of negotiations held in Islamabad, the US made many excessive demands, effectively violating the initial framework, leading to a stalemate in the round of talks. Sources also stated that in the exchanges over the past few days, the US insisted on its excessive demands, which violated the absolute rights of the Iranian people, and the exchanges between the two sides did not achieve any substantial progress. Therefore, Iran finally announced today that, under these circumstances, participating in the negotiations would be a waste of time, as the US is preventing any substantive agreement from being reached. (CCTV) Blockchain Applications ▌Digital Bank Revolut's IPO Valuation Could Reach $200 Billion The Financial Times, citing anonymous investor sources, reported that UK digital bank Revolut plans to seek a valuation of $150 billion to $200 billion in its future IPO, a significant increase from its previous valuation of $75 billion. CEO ONik Storonsky also revealed that Revolut is preparing for a new round of secondary share sales in the second half of 2026, with a valuation potentially exceeding $100 billion. ▌Bezos' AI Lab Reportedly Close to $38 Billion Valuation The Financial Times, citing sources familiar with the matter, reported that Project Prometheus, the AI ​​lab run by Jeff Bezos, is nearing the completion of a new round of financing. The round of funding is estimated at approximately $10 billion, with a post-money valuation of around $38 billion. JPMorgan Chase and BlackRock participated in this round. The deal has not yet been finalized. Project Prometheus is not building general-purpose chatbots. It focuses on AI that can understand the physical world. The goal is to integrate its models into engineering, design, and manufacturing processes. Last month, Axios, citing sources familiar with the matter, stated that the company prefers to focus on prototyping, materials, and early-stage engineering rather than directly replacing factory workers with robots. Cryptocurrency

On April 22, according to on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, Kelp DAO attackers have transferred over 31,000 ETH to tools such as mixers and privacy protocols for laundering, amounting to approximately $72.58 million. In the past 15 hours, two storage addresses have begun making multiple small transactions. Currently, the on-chain addresses retain 75,125 ETH, worth $174 million, and these transactions are still ongoing.

Cryptocurrency

▌KelpDAO attackers have transferred over 31,000 ETH to tools such as mixers and privacy protocols for laundering, amounting to approximately $72.58 million.

▌Tether issues 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network

On April 21, according to Onchainlens monitoring, Tether issued 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. Furthermore, in the past two days, Tether has cumulatively issued 2 billion USDT on the Ethereum network.

▌Coinbase to launch Diem (DIEM) spot trading

On April 22, Coinbase will launch Diem (DIEM) spot trading. If liquidity conditions are met and the trading is available in supported regions, the DIEM-USD trading pair will be launched.

▌Coinbase to Launch Sign (SIGN) Spot Trading

On April 21st, Coinbase will launch spot trading for Sign (SIGN). The SIGN-USD trading pair will open later when liquidity conditions are met, applicable to regions that support the relevant trading.

▌Coinbase to Launch Opengradient (OPG)

On April 21st, according to official news, Coinbase will launch spot trading for Opengradient (OPG).

**Important Economic Developments** **Trump: I Will Be Disappointed If the New Fed Chair Doesn't Cut Rates** **April 21, US President Trump: I will be disappointed if the new Fed chair doesn't cut rates.** **Wash: Agreed to Sell Assets According to Agreement with the Office of Ethics** Fed Chair nominee Warsh: (When asked about his investments) He has agreed to sell assets according to an agreement with the Office of Ethics. **Wash: Independence Depends on the Fed** Fed Chair nominee Warsh: I will be independent of Trump's opinions. Trump tends to demand that the FOMC cut rates. Independence depends on the Fed.

▌Iran Did Not Request an Extension of the Ceasefire; Officials Say US Move Was to Buy Time for a Surprise Attack

On April 22, local time, Iran announced that it had not requested an extension of the ceasefire. Regarding US President Trump's earlier statement on extending the ceasefire, an advisor to the Speaker of the Iranian Islamic Parliament stated on social media, "Trump's proposal to extend the ceasefire is meaningless. The loser has no right to set conditions. Continued blockade and bombing are tantamount to a military response. Moreover, Trump's proposal to extend the ceasefire is clearly to buy time for a surprise attack. Now is the time for Iran to seize the initiative." (CCTV)

▌White House: Vance's Trip to Pakistan Cancelled

On April 22, a White House official said on the 21st that US Vice President Vance's planned trip to Pakistan that day had been cancelled.

According to CNN, the official said in a statement that given President Trump's post on social media "confirming that the United States is awaiting a proposal from Iran, the visit to Pakistan will not take place today." Updates on the face-to-face talks between the US and Iran will be released by the White House. (Xinhua)

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 100%

On April 22, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Fed raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 0%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 100%. The probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 1.7%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 98.3%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin still be called "digital gold" under the geopolitical crisis? Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been substantiated by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: DeFi is caught in the most dangerous prisoner's dilemma in history.
data.description:
More than 40 hours after the theft, the chain reaction triggered by Kelp DAO continues to unfold, involving not only more and more well-known projects such as Aave, LayerZero, and Arbitrum, but even reaching the point where some popular narratives are facing a death sentence.

Well-known KOL Feng Wuxiang stated on the X platform that only ETH is safe now, and ARB has authorized the freezing and transfer of customer assets. There shouldn't be any true L2 anymore. L2 rose with Arbitrum, and it will also die with Arbitrum.

Another well-known KOL, Lanhu, stated that the biggest loser in this Kelp incident was not Aave, nor Kelp, but LayerZero. However, they were too short-sighted and failed to see the true nature of the event. The essence of this event is not that it disproves L2 (let's leave aside fake L2), but that it disproves cross-chain bridges.

More and more heated opinions are emerging in the public sphere, with the parties involved sticking to their own stories and arguing amongst themselves. This makes the Kelp DAO theft a typical window into the division of responsibility for security incidents and the conflict between pragmatism and technological fundamentalism. I. L0 Falsified? Cross-Chain Bridges Become the Biggest Losers The key point of the incident is LayerZero's detailed report on the hacker attack released yesterday, which initially identifies the attackers as the North Korean-backed Lazarus Group. The attack was achieved by poisoning the downstream RPC infrastructure on which its decentralized verifier network (DVN) relies. The attackers controlled some RPC nodes and, in conjunction with a DDoS attack, induced the system to switch to malicious nodes, thereby forging cross-chain transactions. "Using compromised nodes to poison RPC infrastructure, combined with DDoS attacks on unaffected RPCs to force failover, is a highly complex tactic. It's essentially an infrastructure war," commented Samuel Tse, Head of Investments and Partnerships at Animoca Brands. At the end of the report, LayerZero stated that the protocol functioned exactly as expected throughout the incident. No vulnerabilities were found in the protocol. A core feature of the LayerZero architecture is modular security, and in this case, it perfectly achieved its intended goal, isolating the entire attack within a single application—zero risk of infection across the entire system, and other OFT or OApps remained unaffected. This complete abdication of responsibility became the trigger for a huge public backlash, with many prominent industry figures expressing dissatisfaction with LayerZero's performance in this incident. "L0 completely absolves itself of responsibility, blaming everything on KelpDAO's configuration errors in the entire article, while claiming no fault of its own. Unbelievable. Why was a 1/1 configuration allowed? Why was the internal RPC list obtained by the attacker? Why did the failover logic directly trust the poisoned RPC after the DDoS attack, instead of immediately stopping verification or doing anything at all?" Renowned industry researcher CM questioned rhetorically. "This deliberate avoidance makes me very uncomfortable. The statement clearly states that 'the protocol operates exactly as expected.' The attack is described as RPC nodes being compromised and RPC poisoning. But RPC poisoning is not like that; their own infrastructure was compromised and damaged. Given that the statement doesn't explain how the intrusion occurred, I won't rush to re-enable bridging," said renowned DeFi developer banteg. Kelp DAO also issued a statement, clarifying that the single validator (1/1) configuration that led to the attack was not a choice made ignoring advice, but rather the default setting in the official LayerZero guidelines. Furthermore, the validator network (DVN) exploited by the attackers is LayerZero's own infrastructure. According to Dune's analysis, 47% of the 2,665 OApp contracts based on LayerZero use a 1/1 DVN configuration, i.e., a single-validation mechanism, which dramatically amplifies the industry's risks. More alarming than the occurrence of the problem itself is the party's refusal to acknowledge and evade their error. As the leading player in cross-chain communication and the Layer 0 narrative, LayerZero has hundreds of crypto projects using its cross-chain infrastructure to bridge tokens and assets across different chains. If it continues with this arrogant attitude, it will inevitably further erode the industry's confidence in it. Public opinion generally believes that although LayerZero was not directly hacked, its reputation suffered the most damage—it must pay the price for "allowing weak configurations," otherwise the cross-chain narrative will collapse. In other words, LayerZero not only needs to propose clear technical improvements, but also needs to take more responsibility for its asset compensation plan. II. Is Layer2 Dead? Arbitrum's Unconventional Freeze Discussions about Layer2 stem from Arbitrum's freeze. This afternoon, the Arbitrum Security Committee announced that it had taken emergency action to rescue 30,766 ETH, currently worth $71 million, stored by the hackers in the Arbitrum One address. Arbitrum also stated that, after extensive technical investigation and deliberation, the Security Committee identified and implemented a technical solution to transfer funds to a secure location without affecting the state of any other chain or Arbitrum users. The original address holding the funds is no longer able to access them, and only the Arbitrum administration can take further action to transfer these funds, an action that will be coordinated with relevant parties. According to industry interpretations, the Arbitrum Security Committee used a privileged state overriding transaction type (part of ArbOS, but essentially never used) that allows an attacker's private key to still sign transactions, but the ETH at that address is transferred by the chain itself. This special transaction type completely bypasses the attacker's private key and can only be injected by the chain itself (via the sequencer/ArbOS upgrade path, controlled by the Arbitrum Security Committee). It is understood that the Arbitrum Security Committee consists of 12 individuals elected by the Arbitrum DAO, and any decision requires the approval of 9/12 of them. This has caused a great stir. Previously, it was widely believed that Arbitrum, as a representative Layer 2 blockchain, lacked the ability and authority to handle users' ETH assets, as this violated the decentralized spirit of blockchain. In past hacking incidents, stolen USDT and USDC were often frozen immediately by Tether and Circle to minimize user losses. ETH, as a native blockchain asset, has never been frozen and transferred by the blockchain itself, exceeding the expectations of most users. Many viewpoints support Arbitrum's approach, arguing that "all companies, banks, and formal financial institutions will eventually adopt a two-tier architecture. Operating like a centralized entity in critical moments is not a flaw, but an advantage." However, this is not the case for many tech enthusiasts. "No private keys, no authorization, direct transfers." Many believe Arbitrum's move redefines the decentralization of Layer 2, making them feel insecure on Layer 2. Blue Fox bluntly states that this incident directly crosses the core ideological red line of DeFi: "Not Your keys, not your coins." This incident returns to the classic crypto dilemma: pragmatic security vs. fully decentralized security. Conclusion When LayerZero claimed that "the protocol is operating exactly as expected," it preserved technical correctness but lost public opinion and trust. When Arbitrum used privileged transactions to transfer $71 million worth of ETH, it saved user funds but severely damaged the decentralized narrative of Layer2. The Kelp hack brought two of the most popular narratives to the forefront: Are cross-chain bridges infrastructure or risk amplifiers? Is Layer2 a reliable extension of Ethereum or a secondary bank disguised as decentralization? Due to the breach of LayerZero's single validator mechanism, Arbitrum used a centralized special voting mechanism to mitigate the losses for LayerZero and the Kelp DAO. This creates a highly ironic closed loop: a self-proclaimed decentralized protocol collapses due to its "single point of vulnerability," only to ultimately have to rely on the "centralized privileges" of another protocol to survive. This forces the entire industry to confront a question that has never been answered directly: when the ideal of decentralization collides with the security costs of reality, which side are we willing to sacrifice? The grand narrative of the discussion is one focus of public opinion, while the user compensation plan is another, more practical focus. Even though Arbitrum recovered over $70 million through technical means, Aave still has nearly $200 million in bad debts. How can users' interests be properly protected and safeguarded? In most hacking incidents, losses of tens of millions of dollars are devastating for a protocol, and user claims usually end in failure. However, this incident involves leading projects like Aave and Layerzero, making their bad debt handling plans highly anticipated. Aave today proposed two possible bad debt handling solutions. The first is a socialized sharing of losses among all rsETH holders (chain-wide sharing), with Kelp DAO uniformly reducing the value of all rsETH (mainnet + L2) (approximately 15% decoupling). The second is to only have L2-based rsETH holders bear all losses, while mainnet rsETH maintains its original value. However, neither Kelp DAO nor LayerZero has discussed their role in the compensation plan. LayerZero's attempt to absolve itself of responsibility in its report clearly indicates that the project believes there is no obligation to compensate without liability. However, for a protocol with a valuation of billions of dollars, considered the underlying foundation by hundreds of projects, to choose "technical exemption" in the face of massive losses caused by DVN's default configuration is a huge irony of the definition of "underlying infrastructure." This is a classic prisoner's dilemma, where all parties in the crisis are trying to minimize their own losses through "profit-sharing" rather than repairing the industry's trust deficit by sharing responsibility. Judging from the negative impact of this event on all parties in the industry, this will be the most dangerous prisoner's dilemma in the history of DeFi.

Headline

Russian Crypto Exchange Grinex Ceases Operations

On April 21, Grinex, a major Russian cryptocurrency exchange, ceased operations last Wednesday due to a suspected major cyberattack, reportedly incurring losses exceeding 1 billion rubles (approximately $13 million). The platform claimed on Telegram that the attack showed “indications of foreign intelligence agency involvement.” Grinex is the successor to the previously sanctioned and shut-down Garantex exchange, providing the Russian economy with a financial channel to circumvent sanctions. In 2025, it processed nearly $100 billion in trading volume for the sanctioned stablecoin A7A5. Experts point out that Grinex's shutdown is not only due to the hacking attack itself, but more importantly, it removed the trading platform that Russian companies rely on to convert rubles into usable international currencies, causing "serious damage" to Russia's infrastructure for circumventing sanctions and making it more difficult for Russia to escape the pressure of sanctions on the Russian economy. The trading platform was already sanctioned by Western authorities such as the US, UK, and EU in August 2025. Meanwhile, the Russian economy continues to weaken; Putin revealed that GDP fell by 1.8% in January and February this year, and seaborne oil exports may fall to their lowest level since 2023.

    AI Hot Topics

    1. OpenAI Launches Codex Labs

    April 21st news: OpenAI stated that Codex's weekly active users jumped from 3 million to 4 million in two weeks.

    2. Trump: Anthropic is "doing well," willing to consider resuming cooperation

    April 21st - US President Trump stated in an interview with CNBC on Tuesday that his administration believes Anthropic is "doing well" and he is willing to consider reaching an agreement that would allow the company to resume contractual cooperation with Department of Defense officials. Trump instructed federal agencies in February to remove Anthropic from procurement contracts. Anthropic's CEO met with White House officials last week in an attempt to repair relations. The White House described the meeting as productive and constructive.

    ... Trump said on Tuesday, "They came to the White House a few days ago, and we had very in-depth conversations with them. I think they are maturing. They are very smart, and I think they can play an important role. I like smart people." On April 21, according to official news, the 0G Foundation and Alibaba Cloud have reached a cooperation agreement to provide AI agents with direct access to Alibaba's 1000 Questions and Answers series of large language models through blockchain infrastructure. 0G stated that it obtains access credentials for 1000 Questions and Answers through API and embeds them into its own infrastructure, enabling AI agents to directly call the 1000 Questions and Answers models based on a tokenization mechanism, reducing traditional account management, fiat currency billing, and manual configuration processes. This cooperation aims to promote AI from API access to programmable, tokenized infrastructure, supporting AI agents in natural language generation, information processing, decision-making, and multi-step workflow execution.

    4. Justin Sun Makes Online Appearance at HTX Genesis Hackathon: AI Agent is the Core Driving Force for Web3's Advancement to Intelligence

    Justin Sun, Global Advisor of Huobi HTX, made a significant online appearance at the opening ceremony of the HTX Genesis Hackathon in Hong Kong, sharing his latest assessment of the application prospects of AI Agent technology in the Web3 field and the future development direction of the industry. He pointed out that AI Agent will replace traditional interaction methods and become the core driving force of the next stage of the Web3 ecosystem. He explained that past Web3 applications often involved extremely high learning barriers and complex on-chain interactions, while the introduction of AI Agent will drive decentralized applications (DApps) to evolve from "manual operation" to "intention-driven" based on natural language. This means that AI Agent can autonomously understand user needs, automatically plan paths, and complete complex on-chain settlements such as cross-chain transactions.

    ... This paradigm shift towards intelligence and autonomy will not only greatly unleash on-chain productivity but will also become a new breakthrough point for breaking through the bottleneck of Web3 user growth and leading the industry towards true mass adoption. It is reported that the HTX Genesis Hackathon, hosted by HTX DAO and B.AI, and co-hosted by TinTinLand and OpenCity, attracted numerous developer communities, investment institutions, project teams, trading platforms, and public chain ecosystem participants. The 2026 Forbes AI 50 list was released, with OpenAI and Anthropic leading the way. The 2026 Forbes AI 50 list was released, with industry leaders such as OpenAI and Anthropic remaining giants on the list. They have received unprecedented funding from top Silicon Valley venture capital firms and tech giants and are reportedly preparing for a major IPO. These two AI giants have raised a total of $242.6 billion, accounting for approximately 80% of the $305.6 billion raised by the companies on this year's AI 50 list. The widespread adoption of their products has driven strong revenue growth: as of the end of February, OpenAI's annualized revenue reportedly exceeded $25 billion; in early April, Anthropic announced its revenue run rate had surpassed $30 billion. These two AI labs, with products like Claude Code and OpenAI Codex, have become dominant players in markets such as programming, forcing companies like Cursor, valued at $29.3 billion, to innovate for survival.

    The receiving address has not yet been transferred or sold. 2. Aave TVL drops to $16.432 billion, with some funds flowing into Spark. Tether issues 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. On April 21, according to Onchainlens monitoring, Tether issued 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. In addition, Tether has issued a total of 2 billion USDT on the Ethereum network in the past two days.

    4. Aave: WETH Reserves in the Ethereum Core V3 Markets Unfrozen

    On April 21, Aave officially updated the latest developments regarding the rsETH hack: WETH reserves in the Ethereum Core V3 market have been unfrozen, and users can now supply WETH to Ethereum Core V3 again. The LTV (Loan-to-Value Ratio) of WETH remains at 0. WETH reserves on Ethereum Prime, Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, and Linea remain frozen. data.title: The revolutionary fusion of AI Tokens, blockchain Tokens, and fully homomorphic encryption
data.description:
On the afternoon of April 21st, Xiao Feng, Chairman of Wanxiang Blockchain and Chairman and CEO of HashKey Group, delivered a keynote speech entitled "Innovation of Intelligent Agent Economic Model - The Fusion Revolution of AI Token, Blockchain Token and Fully Homomorphic Encryption" at the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, and released the 2026 "Token Economics White Paper". The following is a transcript of the event, with slight modifications that do not affect the original meaning.

Xiao Feng: Good afternoon, everyone!

After yesterday's information "bombardment" and this morning, I believe everyone is quite tired. This afternoon's forum is about RWA. First, we will release the 2026 "Token Economics White Paper".

To recap, HashKey began releasing this series of white papers in 2023.

In the 2024 version, a "three-token model" was specifically proposed, namely equity tokens, utility tokens, and non-fungible tokens such as NFTs. Over the past decade, we've found that a single token might be sufficient for the most basic protocols; however, when targeting applications, B2B and B2C users, a single token layer, such as utility tokens, may not be enough to establish a sound, comprehensive, and effective economic incentive mechanism. Utility tokens are an incentive mechanism for the community, while equity tokens are an incentive mechanism for the entrepreneurial team and shareholders—this was version 1.0 of our "Token Economics White Paper." Now, we've entered version 3.0. This time, we focus on how the intelligent agent economy brought about by AI Agents can be combined with Crypto and blockchain. Therefore, the title of my opening speech today is: Innovation in the Intelligent Agent Economic Model—What Innovation Will the Combination of AI Tokens, Blockchain Tokens, and Privacy Computing Technologies such as ZK and Fully Homomorphic Encryption Bring to the Intelligent Agent Economy? From a business innovation perspective, the main characteristics of blockchain technology can be summarized in two points: First, the blockchain network is a trustless network. This means that no one needs to undergo KYC or sign contracts beforehand; it's an open and transparent network. Trustlessness and permissionlessness are the most significant technical characteristics of blockchain's native business activities. However, this single technical characteristic is clearly insufficient. Another characteristic of blockchain is its transparency. It's difficult to imagine banks or other financial institutions with very high requirements for privacy and compliance putting their entire business process on the blockchain; they would face a huge problem: "data exposure." Neither banks nor other financial institutions, including those with very high privacy requirements like medical data, are likely to operate directly on a digitally native blockchain. However, to date, AI has indeed brought tremendous economic vitality. Especially after AI evolved from large-scale models to AI agents, discussions now focus on how the AI ​​intelligent agent economy can unleash more than ten times the commercial value in the future. However, the same problem arises: data transparency. With completely transparent data, the AI-powered intelligent agent economy has significant flaws, and resolving these flaws requires privacy-preserving computing technology. Looking back at the development of blockchain technology, since the launch of the Bitcoin network in 2009, nearly 16 years of development have proven its enormous commercial and economic value. However, blockchain technology, represented by Bitcoin, does possess the characteristic of openness and transparency. Therefore, in 2015, traditional banks and government regulatory bodies began to propose the concept of "consortium blockchains/permissioned blockchains." The emergence of consortium blockchains and permissioned blockchains stemmed from the fact that the open transparency of public blockchain data prevented many compliant businesses from operating on distributed ledgers. However, while the emergence of permissioned blockchain technology like consortium blockchains alleviated privacy concerns to some extent—only permitted nodes could join and share data within the permitted scope—it also had significant drawbacks. Over the past decade, with the introduction of the consortium blockchain concept, we have seen two very large consortium blockchain organizations: one is R3, a global network of banks, and the other is Hyperledger, led by IBM. Ultimately, it was discovered that after ten years, they hadn't incubated any applications with broad commercial prospects. This led to the view that consortium blockchains might not be blockchains at all. In the context of the time, this did hold some truth. However, with the emergence of tokenization and the tokenization of traditional financial assets, consortium blockchains are making a comeback. We can see that major global banks are already running their own internal permissioned blockchains. However, these internal permissioned blockchains may only have a single node, confirming the blockchain's operation only within the bank—called "private blockchains." So, why is it making a comeback? Because when a globally renowned bank provides its customers with blockchain tokenization services, such as deposit tokenization, it doesn't need to solve the trust issue. It doesn't need a third party as a blockchain node to endorse the trust, because you already trust the bank; you are already its customer. You are simply using tokenization within the bank's account system to complete a remittance from New York to Hong Kong—a cross-border remittance in 2 minutes. Without tokenizing deposits, completing such a remittance could take up to two days, hence the resurgence of private blockchains. However, private blockchains also present a problem: when customers of two different banks conduct cross-border remittances, you still need to traverse the broader network of a single private blockchain. Therefore, consortium blockchains are being discussed again. For example, SWIFT, in conjunction with nine major global banks, is exploring how to use blockchain technology and deposit tokenization tools to solve cross-bank and cross-border fund transfers. This is still under discussion, but we have already seen the resurgence of consortium blockchain technology. However, when it comes to cross-bank transactions, a significant question remains: how much internal bank data should you allow your partners to see? Now, a new technology is emerging: privacy-preserving computation, including zero-knowledge proofs and fully homomorphic encryption. These technologies allow computation while protecting privacy, and the results are completely consistent with the plaintext computation results. These new technologies have actually existed for a while. I remember when Ethereum Devcon was held in Shanghai in 2016, many speakers mentioned privacy computing technologies such as zero-knowledge proofs and formal verification. However, we haven't seen widespread application yet because their performance is insufficient. The cost, time, efficiency, and efficiency of commercial applications don't support them. But what I understand now is that fully homomorphic encryption chips may be launched in the second half of this year, achieving a performance of approximately 1000 transactions per second. This is clearly sufficient for some commercial application scenarios, as some scenarios don't require real-time computation results; you might be willing to wait 10 minutes, half an hour, or an hour. Under fully homomorphic encryption, the integration of blockchain tokens, AI tokens, and fully homomorphic encryption privacy computing technology is necessary to truly create a completely disruptive innovation in the business model of the intelligent agent economy. These three technologies must be combined to achieve disruptive innovation. Meanwhile, if we imagine that zero-knowledge proofs and fully homomorphic encryption algorithms are commercially viable in terms of efficiency, performance, and cost, then blockchain technology may experience another resurgence. At that time, there might not be a need for private or consortium blockchains; all data could be encrypted and stored on public blockchains, and privacy protection technologies would be sufficient to meet the highest levels of global compliance requirements. This is very likely to happen within the next three to five years. Yesterday, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin also spoke on this stage about Ethereum's development path over the next five years, mentioning that Ethereum doesn't need to compete to become the fastest chain, but only to maintain its decentralization and security. Because the "blockchain trilemma" is decentralization, security, and performance, he will address decentralization and security, leaving performance issues to hardware acceleration, algorithm optimization, and L2 and L3 networks to solve according to different application scenarios. I will give an example to illustrate how these three technologies will create new business models. For example, a hospital's medical data is extremely valuable, but its privacy protection requirements are also extremely stringent. In the future token economics model, all commercial institutions will be "token factories." With sufficiently robust zero-knowledge proofs or fully homomorphic encryption privacy computing technology, any hospital can turn its medical data into tokens. Anyone can access this data to calculate desired feature results, but at the same time, they cannot access any personal privacy data. Only when these three technologies are combined can traditional business models be disrupted, forming the ultimate form of the intelligent agent economy. Even with only AI tokens and privacy computing technology, the business logic still holds true; a hospital can still innovate its business model, but the scope of its business will be difficult to expand globally. Digital products and services are essentially global services. Without blockchain support, anyone wanting to use hospital data would need to physically visit the hospital, negotiate, sign agreements, and pay through a bank—clearly not the operating model of a "token factory." What should a "Token Factory" look like? It should utilize the permissionless and trustless nature of blockchain technology to transform all the hospital's data into AI Tokens, making them available to users worldwide. Anyone with needs can access the data 24/7, just like we use the Bitcoin or Ethereum networks now, without signing agreements or undergoing KYC. Accessing data consumes Tokens, and consuming Tokens completes the payment to the hospital. This combination represents the ultimate goal of the intelligent agent economy. The same applies to individuals. If your past years of medical examination and healthcare data are encrypted and stored on the blockchain, you can use a permissionless and trustless business model to request services from global insurance companies: "My encrypted data is here. You can use actuarial models, within a homomorphically encrypted environment, to calculate my data and provide me with the most cost-effective, efficient, and personalized insurance plan." Therefore, with the synergy of these three technologies, the way financial services are conducted will be completely transformed. Insurance brokers or intermediaries will no longer be needed, and you will no longer belong to any single financial institution, yet you will simultaneously be a potential customer of all financial institutions. You can seek your "optimal solution" across the entire network in a trustless and permissionless manner. I also want to emphasize that there is a misconception that AI Tokens are the monetary unit of the AI ​​intelligent agent economy. This is not the case. AI Tokens are the "means of production" of the intelligent agent economy, not a monetary unit. From electricity, chip computing power, large models, algorithms to applications, this is the five-layer structure of Token economics proposed by NVIDIA's Jensen Huang, which actually describes the production process of intelligent agent products or services. However, when you purchase or enjoy products or services, you need to pay with currency, and this currency can only be digital currency. This is because AI Agents cannot use the currency used by humans; they require programmable, subdivisible, and real-time settlement currency. Imagine an AI agent calling an API provided by someone else. It can't operate through a banking system; real-time value settlement is necessary, and each call might only amount to a few cents. Traditional banking payment systems cannot support such small, frequent payments. Therefore, programmable digital currency is the lifeblood of the intelligent agent economy. Furthermore, the intelligent agent economy will generate new digital assets. In this system, both currency and assets need to be tokenized. Otherwise, they cannot be understood or used by machines. Current currencies like the US dollar, Hong Kong dollar, and RMB are not programmable; only after tokenization can they be recognized and used by machines. In summary, the AI ​​intelligent agent economy is a trustless, permissionless business model that will significantly reduce business costs. Society currently operates a massive and expensive system to build business trust and prevent and punish dishonest behavior. This requires the entire society to share the operating costs. However, in trustless and permissionless blockchain business networks, this cost is unnecessary. Under such a low-cost, high-efficiency emerging business model system, new asset classes will emerge. The Crypto world has "native digital assets" like Bitcoin and Ethereum, as well as "twin digital assets" formed after being blockchaind in the real world. Similarly, the AI ​​world will also have native and twin digital assets. AI twin digital assets can be summarized as tokenization; AI native digital assets will emerge in large numbers in the next few years, building entirely new business models and giving rise to a completely new financial service system. Currently, our well-functioning financial service system is designed for "people." But in the future, a new generation of financial service systems, and even entirely new capital market systems, will definitely emerge specifically for AI, machines, and AI native digital assets. That concludes my presentation today. Thank you!

data.description:
Is Agent finally saying goodbye to "going it alone" and ushering in its second stage of evolution? Just this morning, the Dark Side of the Moon officially released and open-sourced the latest flagship model in the Kimi series—the Kimi K2.6. [Areas of time missing] The link provided (link to a webpage) links to the previous version of WeChat Official Account K2.5. The K2.5 version was released less than three months ago. Its release generated a lot of buzz, with the official tweet already reaching 4 million views. Current agents often struggle with complex engineering projects. While they excel at independently completing specific tasks, they lack teamwork skills. Overcoming this limitation is the core objective of Kimi K2.6. The new version explores how to stimulate the team collaboration capabilities of Agents: It further enhances the Agent Swarm (Agent Cluster) function introduced in K2.5, strengthens Agent proactive work through adaptation to frameworks such as OpenClaw, and adds the ability to organize collaboration through the new Claw Group. This systematic combination of capabilities builds an AI system that more closely resembles a human team. To achieve all this, the underlying model must be powerful enough. This time, Kimi K2.6 has made significant progress in core capabilities such as general Agent, code, and graph understanding. In tests like Humanity's Last Exam, SWE-Bench Pro (which closely reflects real-world development scenarios), and DeepSearchQA (which assesses Agent deep retrieval capabilities), K2.6 consistently outperforms its competitors. Even when compared to closed-source models like GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro, K2.6 holds its own, and in some metrics, it even surpasses them. Artificial Analysis has released its latest results, stating that "Kimi K2.6 has become the new king of open-source models!" Following the launch of Kimi K2.6, the large model aggregation platform OpenRouter gave it high praise, believing that the new generation of models from Dark Side of the Moon focuses on long-term time-series programming capabilities and is specifically designed for Agent scenarios that require continuous execution. Compared to traditional chatbots, it is more like a "system engineer," capable of breaking down complex tasks, executing them step by step, and continuously optimizing them in the process. Some netizens exclaimed that this generation of Kimi flagship models is ridiculously powerful. Its coding capabilities are comparable to GPT-5.4, and its price is significantly lower than Opus 4.7. Plus, it's open-source and free to use. Now, roughly every few months, a new open-source model approaches the closed-source GPT and Claude. "It feels like the open-source model has really caught up, and China is driving the pace." (Image of an image: 12-hour shifts, 300 Agents working simultaneously) The ultimate form is here! This time, Kimi K2.6 continues to make strides in the programming field. Just a few days ago, the quietly launched Kimi K2.6-Code-Preview was generating considerable buzz overseas, with high expectations for the official K2.6 release. As the most powerful programming model in the Kimi series to date, Kimi K2.6 has achieved a breakthrough in long-range coding capabilities, which helps to push the automation of software development to a deeper level of engineering. For example, Kimi K2.6 can successfully download and run Qwen3.5-0.8B on a Mac. It doesn't follow the common technology stack; instead, it rewrites the inference process using the less common Zig language and continuously optimizes it. This step itself demonstrates the model's generalization ability. The entire process lasted over 12 hours, involving more than 4000 tool calls and 14 iterations. With continuous parameter tuning and refactoring, the inference speed jumped from approximately 15 tokens/s initially to approximately 193 tokens/s, ultimately becoming about 20% faster than the local large-model chat application LM Studio. Next, we come to the core of this Kimi K2.6 upgrade, which continues and further enhances the collaborative output capabilities of the Agent cluster. Simply put, this feature aims to streamline "how Agents work together". To what extent can it achieve this now? K2.6 automatically breaks down a complex task and assigns it to agents with different specializations, allowing them to handle tasks such as searching, in-depth research, document analysis, and long-form writing, and then piecing the results together to continue the process. Under this mechanism, a single run can complete the entire process: from raw data and web page content to PPTs and tables, everything is automatically generated without switching between tools or requiring manual intervention. Simultaneously, the underlying architecture of the Agent cluster has also been expanded, enabling the simultaneous scheduling of up to 300 sub-Agents to complete 4000 collaborative steps, significantly boosting parallel processing capabilities to a new level. With this increased scale, the role of AI has changed: it begins to take over the entire process and directly provide systematic results. The Agent cluster disassembled and reused an astrophysical paper containing high-density visual data, generating a research report of approximately 7,000 words, 20,000 dataset entries, and 14 charts. To enable AI to evolve into a 24/7, uninterrupted cyber employee requiring no human intervention, Kimi K2.6 has undergone deeper adaptation to frameworks such as OpenClaw and Hermes Agent. To this end, Kimi K2.6 further pushes the limits of the model's autonomous execution capabilities: whether it's the accuracy of API calls, the stability of long-term operation, or the security protection when performing complex research tasks, K2.6 performs admirably. In terms of Vibe Coding, Kimi K2.6's website design is even more outstanding. The websites generated by K2.6, especially the first screen, have a strong visual impact at first glance, and the style consistency is well maintained. Furthermore, the addition of various interactive elements and scrolling effects helps to keep users on the site for longer. Besides the front-end design, Kimi K2.6 also brings surprises to back-end developers by launching Kimi account login and form information collection functions. You can use it to create an event registration page and easily view backend registration information. This makes the front-end and back-end integration smoother. Currently, Kimi K2.6 has become the default model for the Kimi web version, app, and Kimi Code programming assistant. Start using it now!

First-hand testing, showcasing the best

Without further ado, let's get straight to some hands-on testing with some examples and see how it performs.
